content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Bind strict source, declarations, maps, generated fixtures, package exports, emitted JavaScript, and startup entry to one revision.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Source Emission Map model · Theory Source Emissi…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 2508 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile Node, TypeScript, pinned crawler packages, emitted artifacts, clean gates, and recovery anchors; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bind strict source, declarations, maps, generated fixtures, package exports, emitted JavaScript, and startup entry to one revision.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Source Emission Map model · Theory Source Emissi…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A noEmit type-check proves shipped JavaScript exists and runs.
